title: openQA MCP server (fastmcp + openqa-async)
description:
Build an MCP server exposing curated tools over the openQA REST API, using fastmcp 3.x and the openqa-async thin transport client (openqa_request(method, path, ...)).

Locked decisions:
- Curated high-level typed tools (not a generic passthrough).
- Auth via ~/.config/openqa/client.conf (HMAC-SHA1), with our own OPENQA_SERVER / OPENQA_API_KEY / OPENQA_API_SECRET env fallback (openqa-async itself does NOT read env vars).
- Transport: stdio by default, optional HTTP.
- Scope: server + smoke tests + README.

Key facts:
- openqa-async is a thin transport: sole public method is openqa_request(method, path, params=, data=, json=) returning parsed JSON/YAML.
- Credentials read only from /etc/openqa/client.conf and ~/.config/openqa/client.conf; no key/secret => GET-only.
- REST base: /api/v1. Routes: jobs, jobs/overview, jobs/{id}, jobs/{id}/comments, jobs/{id}/restart (POST), jobs/{id}/cancel (POST), machines, test_suites, products, isos (POST), job_settings/jobs.
- fastmcp 3.4.3: FastMCP(name, instructions=...), @mcp.tool on async fns, mcp.run(transport='stdio'|'http', host=, port=).

Success criteria:
- uv sync installs cleanly; uv run openqa-mcp starts stdio MCP server.
- fastmcp inspect lists all curated tools with valid schemas.
- uv run pytest passes (list_jobs params, None-dropping, a POST tool, env-cred X-API-Key).
- OPENQA_MCP_TRANSPORT=http binds host/port and answers initialize.
- Against a real instance, get_job/list_machines return live data; README steps reproduce setup.

title: Scaffold package + configure pyproject (src layout, entry point, dev deps)
description:
Move to a src-layout package 'openqa_mcp' and wire packaging.

Tasks:
- Add [project.scripts] openqa-mcp = 'openqa_mcp.server:main'.
- Configure hatchling (or uv default) to build the src/openqa_mcp package.
- Add dev dependency group: pytest, pytest-asyncio, respx, httpx.
- Keep requires-python >=3.13.
- Create src/openqa_mcp/ directory.

Verify:
- uv sync succeeds.
- uv run openqa-mcp --help resolves the entry point (may error until server.py exists; entry point must at least be discoverable).
close_reason: Scaffolded src-layout package: uv_build backend, openqa-mcp entry point (openqa_mcp.server:main), dev group (pytest, pytest-asyncio, respx, httpx). uv sync succeeds, lock de-virtualized to editable, entry point discoverable (fails only on missing server.py per cw0.4).

title: Add package marker src/openqa_mcp/__init__.py with __version__
description: Add __version__ to src/openqa_mcp/__init__.py. NOTE: the empty __init__.py file was already created in cw0.1 because uv_build requires the module marker to build; this task now just populates __version__ content.

title: Implement client factory + lifespan (client.py) with env-cred override
description:
src/openqa_mcp/client.py: build and manage the shared AsyncOpenQAClient.

Tasks:
- get_client(): read OPENQA_SERVER (else openqa-async default), OPENQA_VERIFY; construct AsyncOpenQAClient(server=..., verify=...).
- If OPENQA_API_KEY / OPENQA_API_SECRET env vars are set: override client._apikey and client.apisecret, then REBUILD client.client (httpx.AsyncClient auth + headers) so env creds actually take effect (constructor bakes auth at init time). Isolate this library-internal touch-point with a clear comment.
- Provide a single shared client via FastMCP lifespan: open on startup, await client.aclose() on shutdown.

Risk: touches openqa-async internals (_apikey / rebuilding client.client). Isolate in this module; prefer client.conf in docs.

Verify:
- Unit test asserts env creds land in the built httpx client's headers (X-API-Key) and auth.

title: Implement curated MCP tools (server.py) over openqa_request
description:
src/openqa_mcp/server.py: mcp = FastMCP('openQA', instructions=...). Register curated typed async tools, each a thin wrapper over openqa_request that drops None params and returns parsed dict/list. One-line docstring per tool (becomes MCP description).

READ tools:
- list_jobs(state,result,distri,version,build,test,arch,machine,groupid,group,latest,limit,page,ids) -> GET jobs
- list_jobs_overview(...) -> GET jobs/overview (same filter subset)
- get_job(job_id:int) -> GET jobs/{id}
- get_job_comments(job_id:int) -> GET jobs/{id}/comments
- list_machines() -> GET machines
- list_test_suites() -> GET test_suites
- list_products() -> GET products
- find_jobs_by_setting(key, list_value) -> GET job_settings/jobs

MUTATING tools (require creds; 403 without), tagged {'mutating'}:
- restart_jobs(job_ids:list[int]) -> POST jobs/restart (or per-id jobs/{id}/restart)
- cancel_job(job_id:int) -> POST jobs/{id}/cancel
- add_job_comment(job_id:int, text:str) -> POST jobs/{id}/comments
- trigger_isos(distri,version,flavor,arch,**extra) -> POST isos

Verify:
- uv run fastmcp inspect src/openqa_mcp/server.py lists all tools with valid input schemas.

title: Add main() entry with transport selection (stdio default, optional HTTP)
description:
Add main() in server.py to select transport.

Tasks:
- Default: mcp.run() (stdio).
- If OPENQA_MCP_TRANSPORT=http (or --http flag): mcp.run(transport='http', host=OPENQA_MCP_HOST, port=OPENQA_MCP_PORT).
- Minimal arg/env parsing only.

Verify:
- stdio starts and answers MCP initialize (fastmcp inspect).
- HTTP mode binds the configured host/port.

title: Remove placeholder main.py (superseded by package entry point)
description:
Delete the placeholder main.py now that openqa-mcp = 'openqa_mcp.server:main' is the entry point.

Verify:
- uv run openqa-mcp still works after deletion.

title: Write smoke tests (pytest + respx, in-memory FastMCP client)
description:
tests/test_tools.py + tests/__init__.py. Mock httpx at transport level with respx (no live openQA). Invoke tools end-to-end via FastMCP in-memory client.

Cover:
- list_jobs builds GET /api/v1/jobs with expected query params and returns parsed JSON.
- None params are dropped from the query string.
- A mutating tool issues the correct POST (path + body).
- Env-cred override sets X-API-Key on the outgoing request.

Verify:
- uv run pytest is green.

title: Write README (install, config, tool list, MCP client wiring)
description:
README.md documenting:
- Install: uv sync.
- Config: ~/.config/openqa/client.conf example ([host] key/secret), plus OPENQA_SERVER / OPENQA_API_KEY / OPENQA_API_SECRET / OPENQA_VERIFY env vars.
- Tool list (read + mutating) with brief descriptions.
- Wiring into an MCP client: stdio command (uv run openqa-mcp) and optional HTTP (OPENQA_MCP_TRANSPORT=http + host/port).

Verify:
- Commands in the README run as written.
